🏃 Health and Physical Education

Drills and Physical Training

Learn discipline and fitness through drills and physical training!

📚 Unit 6 ⏱️ 9 min read

Unit 6: Drills and Physical Training

Drills and physical training (PT) help us become disciplined, fit, and strong. They are important parts of school life.

Lesson 1: Drills

What are Drills?

Drills are organized movements performed in a group, usually following commands. They teach discipline, teamwork, and coordination.

Importance of Drills

BenefitExplanation
DisciplineLearn to follow orders immediately
TeamworkMove together as one unit
ConcentrationFocus on commands and actions
Body postureStand and walk correctly
ConfidenceFeel proud performing together
SafetyLearn to assemble quickly

Basic Drill Commands

CommandAction
AttentionStand straight, heels together, toes apart, hands at sides
Stand at easeMove left foot slightly apart, hands behind back
Sit downSit cross-legged in one motion
Stand upStand quickly without using hands
Right turnTurn 90 degrees to the right
Left turnTurn 90 degrees to the left
About turnTurn 180 degrees (face the opposite direction)
March forwardWalk forward in step
HaltStop marching immediately
DismissLeave the formation

Proper Standing Positions

Attention Position

Body partPosition
HeadStraight, looking forward
ChestOut
ShouldersBack
ArmsStraight down, fingers together
LegsHeels together, toes at 45 degrees
WeightEvenly on both feet

Stand at Ease Position

Body partPosition
Left footMoved about 12 inches to the left
HandsClasped behind the back
BodyRelaxed but straight

Drill Formations

FormationDescription
Line (Row)Standing side by side in one line
ColumnStanding one behind the other
SquareForming a square shape
CircleStanding in a circle

Marching

Marching is walking in a disciplined, rhythmic way.

CommandAction
Quick marchStart marching at normal pace
Double marchStart running on the spot (then forward)
Left/right wheelTurn the formation left or right while marching
Mark timeMarch in place (without moving forward)

💡 Tip: When marching, move the left arm forward with the right leg, and the right arm forward with the left leg.

Lesson 2: Physical Training (PT)

What is Physical Training (PT)?

Physical Training (PT) is a set of exercises that improve strength, flexibility, endurance, and overall fitness.

Importance of PT

BenefitExplanation
Strong musclesBuilds muscle strength
Healthy heartImproves blood circulation
Good postureCorrects slouching
EnergyMakes you feel active
Weight controlBurns extra calories
Stress reliefReduces tension and anxiety
Better sleepTired body sleeps well

Warm-Up Exercises

Why warm up? Warming up prepares the body for exercise and prevents injury.

Warm-up ExerciseHow to do itDuration
Neck rotationSlowly rotate neck left and right, up and down30 seconds
Shoulder rotationRotate shoulders forward and backward30 seconds
Arm circlesMake circles with arms30 seconds
Side bendsBend body to left and right30 seconds
Torso twistsTwist upper body left and right30 seconds
Leg swingsSwing leg forward and backward30 seconds
Ankle rotationRotate ankles clockwise and anticlockwise30 seconds
On-the-spot joggingJog without moving forward1 minute

Basic PT Exercises

1. Jumping Jacks

StepAction
1Stand straight, arms at sides
2Jump and spread legs apart
3At the same time, raise arms above head
4Jump back to starting position
Repeat10-20 times

2. Squats

StepAction
1Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
2Bend knees as if sitting on a chair
3Keep back straight
4Return to standing
Repeat10-15 times

3. Push-ups (Modified for beginners)

StepAction
1Lie face down, hands under shoulders
2Keep knees on the ground
3Push body up until arms are straight
4Lower body back down
Repeat5-10 times

4. Sit-ups (Crunches)

StepAction
1Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat
2Place hands behind head
3Lift upper body toward knees
4Lower back down
Repeat10-15 times

5. Lunges

StepAction
1Stand straight
2Step forward with right leg
3Lower body until both knees are bent at 90 degrees
4Push back to starting position
5Repeat with left leg
Repeat5-10 on each leg

6. Plank

StepAction
1Lie face down
2Lift body onto forearms and toes
3Keep body straight like a plank
4Hold the position
Duration15-30 seconds

Stretching Exercises (Cool Down)

Why cool down? Stretching after exercise prevents muscle soreness.

StretchHow to do it
Hamstring stretchSit, extend one leg, reach for toes
Quadriceps stretchStand, pull heel toward buttocks
Triceps stretchRaise one arm, bend elbow behind head
Chest stretchClasp hands behind back, open chest
Butterfly stretchSit, bring soles of feet together, press knees down

PT Commands

CommandAction
Fall inLine up in formation
Fall outLeave the formation
Stand at easeRelaxed standing position
AttentionStand straight and still
Ready, steady, goStart the exercise
StopStop exercising

PT Etiquette (Rules)

RuleWhy
Wear proper sports clothes and shoesSafety and comfort
Listen to the instructorTo do exercises correctly
Do not push or shovePrevent injuries
Drink water during breaksStay hydrated
Stop if you feel painAvoid injury
Help friends who struggleTeamwork

Sample PT Session (30 minutes)

ActivityDuration
Warm-up exercises5 minutes
Jumping jacks2 minutes
Squats3 minutes
Push-ups2 minutes
Sit-ups2 minutes
Lunges2 minutes
Plank1 minute
Running on the spot3 minutes
Cool-down stretches5 minutes
Rest and water break5 minutes

💡 Remember: Do PT regularly – at least 3-4 times a week!

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Drills teach ______ and teamwork.
  2. In attention position, heels are ______ and toes are apart.
  3. A 180-degree turn is called an ______ turn.
  4. ______ exercises prepare the body for physical activity.
  5. After exercise, we should do ______ stretches.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. We should skip warm-up before exercise. (T/F)
  2. Marching uses opposite arm and leg movement. (T/F)
  3. PT helps build strong muscles. (T/F)
  4. We should drink water during exercise breaks. (T/F)
  5. Plank is a stretching exercise. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Name the Exercise

Describe the exercise.

  1. Jump and spread legs while raising arms overhead → ______
  2. Lie face down and push body up → ______
  3. Bend knees like sitting on a chair → ______
  4. Hold body straight on forearms and toes → ______

Exercise 4: Match the Command to the Action

CommandAction
Attentiona) Relax with hands behind back
Stand at easeb) Stop marching
Haltc) Stand straight and still
Dismissd) Leave the formation

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. discipline, 2. together, 3. about, 4. Warm-up, 5. cool-down

Exercise 2: 1. F, 2. T, 3. T, 4. T, 5. F

Exercise 3: 1. Jumping jacks, 2. Push-ups, 3. Squats, 4. Plank

Exercise 4: Attention→c, Stand at ease→a, Halt→b, Dismiss→d

Great work! Stay fit, stay disciplined, and enjoy physical training every day!
